notes: The theorem statement requires the second Jacobi-Trudi formula using elementary symmetric functions and transpose partitions. The visible context provides definitions for NPartition, schur, jacobiTrudiMatrixH (using hsymmExt), and related lemmas, but lacks: (1) a definition of the elementary symmetric function esymm in the same polynomial ring MvPolynomial (Fin N) R (the Mathlib MvPolynomial.esymm exists but requires Fintype σ, which Fin N satisfies; however, the index in the formula is λ^t_i - μ^t_j - i + j, which may be negative, so an extension esymmExt analogous to hsymmExt is needed), (2) a definition of the transpose of an NPartition (the project has NPartition.transpose but it is defined in a different file and may not be imported; the visible context does not include it), (3) a definition of the skew Schur function s_{λ/μ} (the visible context has schur for non-skew shapes only; the source references def.sf.skew-schur but it is not in the visible context), (4) the first Jacobi-Trudi formula (jacobiTrudi_h) which would be used as a lemma, but it is not present in the visible context (only jacobiTrudiMatrixH is defined, but the theorem stating the determinant equals the skew Schur is missing). Without these definitions and theorems, the proof cannot be completed from the visible context.
